originalTitle: DR Congo takes Rwanda to international court over decades of conflict
title: Congo and Rwanda Talk in a Big, Special Court!
content:
Hello, hello, amazing kids across our beautiful Africa! It's your friend, Reporter Kito, bringing you big news from our continent! Today, we're talking about two countries that are neighbours, just like you might have a friend living next door. These countries are the Democratic Republic of Congo, or DR Congo for short, and Rwanda.

Big News from Our Neighbours!

So, what's the big news? Well, DR Congo has decided to go to a very special, very big court. It's like a special classroom for grown-up countries! They are asking this court to help them talk about some problems they have had with their neighbour, Rwanda. It's about things that have been difficult between them for a very, very long time.

Meet DR Congo and Rwanda

Let's first get to know these two countries. DR Congo is a super, super big country in the middle of Africa! It has huge rainforests with amazing trees and special animals you can only find there, like the okapi! Did you know? The okapi looks a bit like a zebra and a giraffe mixed together! Many people live in DR Congo, and they speak different languages like Lingala and Swahili. Its capital city is called Kinshasa. Right next to DR Congo is Rwanda. Rwanda is a smaller country, but it's very beautiful! People call it the 'Land of a Thousand Hills' because there are so many green, rolling hills. The people in Rwanda mostly speak Kinyarwanda. Their capital city is called Kigali.

What's the Problem Between Neighbours?

Just like you and your friends might sometimes have an argument, countries can have big problems too. DR Congo is saying that Rwanda has done some things that were not kind or helpful for many, many years. These problems have made it difficult for people in DR Congo to live peacefully and safely. It's important for countries to be good neighbours, so everyone can play, learn, and grow up happily. When there are problems, it's like a big cloud making everyone feel sad or scared.

A Very Sad Time in the Past (1994)

Many years ago, even before many of you were born, something very, very sad happened in Rwanda. It was in 1994. During that time, many, many people in Rwanda got hurt, and a lot of families lost their loved ones. It was called a genocide. This means a big group of people tried to hurt another big group of people just because they were different. It was a terrible time, and it left many scars. After this sad time, things stayed difficult for a long time in the whole region. DR Congo says that some of the problems they are having with Rwanda now started around that time and have continued until today. They want the big court to listen to their story.

What is a Special Big Court?

Imagine you and your friend have a big argument that you can't solve on your own. Sometimes, a grown-up, like your teacher or your mum or dad, helps you talk it out fairly. They listen to both sides and try to find a way for everyone to be friends again, right? Well, this special court is like a super big grown-up for countries! It's called the International Court of Justice. Did you know? This court is in a city called The Hague, which is very far away in a country called the Netherlands! Inside this court, there are very smart people from all over the world. They are called judges. These judges listen to what DR Congo says and what Rwanda says. They try to understand everything very carefully to help make things fair and safe for everyone.

Why Go to This Court Now?

DR Congo wants to tell the judges all about the problems it believes Rwanda has caused. They want the court to say that Rwanda needs to stop doing these things and help make things better. It's like asking for a big grown-up to help make sure everyone plays by the rules and treats each other with respect. They hope that by talking in this court, they can find a way to make peace between the two countries. Peace means that people can live without fear, children can go to school, play with their friends, and families can be safe in their homes. Peace is a wonderful thing that everyone deserves! Did you know? Our continent, Africa, has 54 different countries! Imagine how many neighbours that is! It's so important for all these neighbours to try and live together peacefully.

What Happens Next?

The judges at the International Court of Justice will now listen very carefully to everything both DR Congo and Rwanda have to say. It can take some time, just like it takes time to fix a big puzzle. After listening to everyone, the judges will think very hard. They will try to find the fairest way to help both countries live together safely and happily. We all hope that this big talk will lead to a better, more peaceful future for everyone in DR Congo and Rwanda. Reporter Kito, wishing for peace and friendship for all our amazing African countries! Stay tuned for more news!
summary: DR Congo has asked a special world court to help resolve long-standing problems with its neighbour, Rwanda, hoping to find a way for both countries to live in peace.
whyMatters: This is important because when countries are not friends, it can make people scared and unhappy. Talking in a big court is a way for grown-ups to try and fix problems peacefully so everyone can be safe and happy.
